items.description:
[[home event]]
The Twin Cities Wyverns' 2025 Season opener! Come through and warm up your winter with delicious brews and exciting action! 

Tickets here

Participants:

Sign ups are closed.

Schedule:
 - Call time & setup: 1pm
- Kit up by 2:30pm and do warm ups for early crowd
- Show 3pm - 6pm:  Event organizer wants 'continuous fighting' - we'll be doing 2 fights every quarter hour for the 3 hour period with some free-for-alls thrown in for fun. Fights will be scheduled such that you'll get good breaks and and an opportunity to explore the festival and try some beer.
- Teardown asap after show, trailer can be onsite at 7, out by 8.


Notes:
- We will have a fairly large, private locker room close to the list, and a 2nd one not much farther if we need it
- Nearby water kegs and EMS, security
- limited parking, will need to plan carpools
- lots of food trucks, can enjoy the beer
- Limited # of passes means limited # of support.
- Need to be on extra good behavior - many eyes on us - especially want to make a good impression with Dabbler organizers and State Fair hosts.
items.location: MN State Fairgrounds, Hoyt Ave W, Falcon Heights, MN 55108, USA

items.description:
New member practices are conducted each quarter and provide an introduction to our team and the sport for our new members. If you're interested in joining our team and attending this practice please fill out the intake form and details will be provided via email. 

Contact linden.holt@mn-armored-combat.org with any questions

Agenda:
- Resolve Carnage expenses

[[away event]]

WisMCC and The Milwaukee Iron Stags present Medieval Mayhem, a night of medieval tournament style fighting. We are in a new venue this time around and working with Soundcheck Entertainment to bring you our best show yet!

This event displays the sport of armored combat, a non-scripted fully competitive sport based around medieval tournament fighting. You’ll see real steel weapons and armor on display as the competitors fight for victory and your admiration!

Located at Shotzy’s 2, 6922 W Orchard St. West Allis, WI 53214. The event will take place on April 5th 2025 from 8:00-10:00pm with doors opening at 7:00PM. Tickets are $30 each and can be purchased through the Eventbrite link.

Tickets

Facebook Event
items.location: Shotzy's Country Rock Bar 2, 6922 W Orchard St, Milwaukee, WI 53214, USA

items.description:
[[away event]]

The Wyverns return for the 3rd year in a row to one of our favorite ren faires - the Colorado Medieval Festival!

Participants:

This event is always a blast. The Wardens have some top-tier fighters on both the melee and duels side so the quality of fights is very high. 

2 days, mix of demo and light tournament fighting. Duels & melees. Will be a Buhurt International event. Camping options on-site.

Participant sign-ups are closed.

[[home event]]


The Doobie Dabbler is the end-of-summer adventure returning in 2025 to Sever’s festival grounds, spanning 100 acres.

With a blend of local Minnesotan culture and [redacted], the two-day open-air event features 30+ unique activities, including one of the top corn mazes in the country, multiple live music stages, a Giant Slide, BMX, and an amazing new lineup of activations and experiences including Petting Zoo, Pig Races, Scavenger Hunt Hayride (and the Twin Cities Wyverns beating the heck out of each other for your entertainment)

Participant Sign-ups

items.description:
Practice for MNAC's women's division team, The Flowers of Battle. Open to all femme-identifying individuals. 

Practice usually consists of a mobility and conditioning routine, following by striking/grappling drills and instruction. 

Contact amy.jo.hopfensperger@mn-armored-combat.org with any questions.

Practice usually consists of a mobility and conditioning routine, following by striking/grappling drills and instruction. 

Contact amy.jo.hopfensperger@mn-armored-combat.org with any questions.

Sunday practice is only open to MNAC members. It is conducted out of steel, and focuses on buhurt fundamentals. Bring workout clothes and a water bottle.

Practice usually consists of a mobility routine, 30 minute warmup/cardio, 30 minutes of grappling drills, and 30 minutes of soft-kit sparring. 

Contact linden.holt@mn-armored-combat.org with any questions.

Advanced practice is open to all dues-paying MNAC members in good standing. 

Advanced practice conducted in soft kit, and is focused on dynamic practice of techniques with a focus on high intensity and high reps. Minimal instruction is given and its assumed that attendees have a basic grasp of buhurt concepts.

Practice for MNAC's women's division team, The Flowers of Battle. Open to all femme-identifying individuals. 

Practice usually consists of a mobility and conditioning routine, following by striking/grappling drills and instruction. 

Contact amy.jo.hopfensperger@mn-armored-combat.org with any questions.

Saturday steel practice is open to all dues-paying MNAC members in good standing. 

Steel practice is focused on 1v1 formats, advanced melee skills, and sparring. 

Contact linden.holt@mn-armored-combat.org with any questions.

Advanced practice is open to all dues-paying MNAC members in good standing. 

Advanced practice conducted in soft kit, and is focused on dynamic practice of techniques with a focus on high intensity and high reps. Minimal instruction is given and its assumed that attendees have a basic grasp of buhurt concepts.

Profight practice is open to all dues-paying MNAC members in good standing. 

Lead by AMCF middleweight champion Alex King:

"We would start with fundamentals in soft kit and progress from there. Each practice will build on the last (each week will have a new terminal learning objective) with the goal of transitioning to hard kit practical application of skills. 

The ultimate goal would be to prepare you for next seasons profights and developing your own individual style of fighting. 

If you have interest in profighting it would benefit you to try to make it to all."
